etymology.description: semantic 金(金) (jīn, 'metal; gold') + phonetic 羅(羅) (luó) -> meaning
etymology.interpretation: 鑼(鑼) (luó, 'gong') combines 金(金) (jīn, 'metal; gold'), indicating that it is a metal object, with 羅(羅) (luó), which contributes the sound. The character represents a metal musical instrument that produces sound, aligning with its meaning as a 'gong'.
etymology.simplification: 鑼(鑼) was simplified to 锣(鑼) by replacing the complex 羅(羅) (luó) with the simpler component 夸(夸) (kuā, 'boast'), which retains the phonetic 'luó' sound while reducing stroke count.

examples.chinese: 村里每当有节日就会敲锣打鼓(村里每當有節日就會敲鑼打鼓)。
examples.pinyin: Cūn lǐ měi dāng yǒu jiérì jiù huì qiāo luó dǎ gǔ.
examples.english: Every time there is a festival in the village, people beat gongs and drums.

examples.chinese: 他们用力敲打锣(他們用力敲打鑼)，庆祝新年的到来(慶祝新年的到來)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen yònglì qiāo dǎ luó, qìngzhù xīnnián de dàolái.
examples.english: They struck the gong vigorously to celebrate the arrival of the New Year.

examples.chinese: 李白在诗中描述了市集中锣声的喧闹(李白在詩中描述了市集中鑼聲的喧鬧)。
examples.pinyin: Lǐ Bái zài shī zhōng miáoshù le shìjí zhōng luó shēng de xuānnào.
examples.english: Li Bai described the clamor of gongs in the market in his poetry.
